turns.user_message: They made a passing comment about how I always overthink things, and it just sat in my chest all afternoon. I used to laugh that off and brush it aside without a second thought. Now it feels like every little remark carries a hidden weight I have to carry around. I keep replaying it in my head, wondering what else I am missing. It is exhausting trying to figure out where I went wrong.

Every time they sigh when I ask a simple question, I feel this heavy knot tighten in my stomach. That kind of reaction never used to get under my skin, but lately it happens constantly. I find myself walking on eggshells just to avoid triggering another quiet disappointment. I keep shrinking my own needs to keep the peace, but it leaves me feeling completely hollow. The silence between us feels louder than any actual argument ever could.

When they finally told me I was just too much to handle, something inside me completely snapped. I used to brush off their blunt honesty as just their straightforward way of talking, but now it cuts straight through me. I am drowning in a constant loop of questioning every single word I say. I feel like I am losing my footing in a room I used to know so well. The weight of trying to be acceptable is just crushing me from the inside out.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
